scope_boundary: GitHub Actions publication-pipeline metadata only: recent Pages run durations, classified workflow failures, and repair boundaries. This is not production workload evidence, scheduler behavior, economic optimization, model quality, or physical-feasibility evidence.
transition_reason: The CI cost profile makes local publication pipeline costs visible and records a failure/repair boundary, but the measured system is GitHub Pages publication for the book. It therefore blocks promotion for any claim about deployed resource scheduling, economic outcomes, runtime enforcement, model quality, or production workload quality.
required_artifacts: scheduler or queue logs outside publication CI
negative_results: failure repair is not treated as scheduler quality
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: publication metadata described as production scheduler evidence
failure repair described as economic optimization
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed resource-cost claim after production or externally reviewed workload records, scheduler logs, runtime budget-enforcement measurements, human-productivity or economic review, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent operations, scheduler, economics, or benchmarking audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove production scheduler behavior
does not prove economic optimization, model quality, runtime enforcement, or physical feasibility
does not create an upward support-state transition
